# Four Thousand Weeks - Life Calendar

> A Memento Mori life calendar that visualizes a human lifespan as a grid of approximately 4,000 weeks.

Inspired by Oliver Burkeman's book "Four Thousand Weeks: Time Management for Mortals" and the Stoic practice of Memento Mori — "remember that you will die."

## What It Does

- Displays an 80-year lifespan as an interactive 52×80 grid (52 weeks per year, 80 years)
- Each cell represents one week of life
- Color-coded cells show weeks lived, the current week, and future weeks
- Users can add life events and eras (education, work, relationships, etc.) to annotate their timeline
- Fully client-side — all data stays in the browser via localStorage (no server, no tracking)

## Key Features

- Interactive week grid with hover tooltips and click-to-add events
- Life eras with color coding (work, education, location, relationship, health)
- Point-in-time life events with custom labels
- Statistics: weeks lived, weeks remaining, percentage of life
- Dark and light mode (follows system preference)
- Print-optimized layout for physical display
- Data import/export as JSON

## Tech Stack

React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, Zustand (state), date-fns (dates), Zod (validation)

## Links

- Live App: https://four-thousand-weeks.chayuto.com/
- Source Code: https://github.com/chayuto/four-thousand-weeks
- Full Documentation for LLMs: https://four-thousand-weeks.chayuto.com/llms-full.txt
